info.title: Seren Agent
info.description: Orchestrate agent deployments through the seren-agent publisher.

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Optional private model region for live discovery. Defaults to us-east-1.

post.summary: Run an unsaved managed seren-agent draft once before deploying.

ActionLease.description:
Lease for a single action on a tool. Borrows the OpenFang/IronClaw
pattern: permission is granted per-action, not per-process, with optional
expiry, use budget, and a parent lease ref for sub-agent inheritance.

`capability` distinguishes blanket grants (all sub-actions of `action`)
from a closed `Specific` set. Sub-agents that fork from a parent must
hold leases whose capability is a subset of the parent's — runtime
enforcement (when implemented) lives outside this DTO; here we validate
shape only.

action.description:
Action name being leased. Free-form per tool; e.g. `read`, `send`,
`execute`, `delete`, `list`.

expiry.description: Lease expiry as RFC 3339; `None` means no time bound.

parent_lease_ref.description:
Identifier of the parent lease this one inherits from. Shape-only
validation here checks that the value is non-empty when set; the
referenced lease is not looked up. Inheritance resolution (parent must
hold a capability superset) happens at runtime.

use_budget.description:
Maximum number of invocations before the lease must be re-issued.
`None` means unlimited.

AgentAssetFile.description: Non-prompt file that belongs to an agent bundle.

content_type.description: Optional MIME type supplied by the caller.

oneOf.description: Optional caller-supplied purpose for UI grouping and runtime materialization.

sha256.description: Optional content digest for provenance. When present, the server verifies it.

AgentBundle.description: Authored agent bundle used by LLM workloads.

assets.description: Additional files available to the agent runtime.

instructions.description:
Prompt-bearing instruction files rendered server-side into the runtime
system prompt.

AgentBundlePatch.description: Partial agent-bundle update for an existing managed deployment.

remove_assets.description: Asset paths to remove before applying upserts.

remove_instructions.description: Instruction files to remove before applying upserts.

upsert_assets.description: Asset files to insert or replace. Identity is `path`.

upsert_instructions.description: Instruction files to insert or replace. Identity is `(kind, path)`.

AgentCapabilityPolicy.description:
Optional runtime capabilities declared by a managed agent.

This is distinct from `tool_refs`: capability policy controls bundled
runtime behavior such as tool-call error recovery and optional tool
families that require runtime prerequisites.

AgentCredentialRef.description:
Reference to a credential held by Seren's control plane or by the
organization credential-secret API, injected at the declared binding point.

binding.description:
How the credential is delivered. `proxy_inject` is declarative: the
gateway resolves and injects the reference at egress rather than this
process materializing plaintext.

name.description: Name the workload uses to address the credential.

ref_uri.description:
URI of the credential record. Supported schemes:
`control-plane://providers/{id}`, `org-secret://{key}`,
`user-secret://{key}`, `seren-secrets://{vault}/{item}/{field}`.
Create `org-secret` and `user-secret` records with
`/organizations/{organization_id}/agent-credential-secrets`.

enforcement.description: How strictly the runtime should enforce these paths.

read_only_paths.description: Absolute paths the workload may read but not write.

read_write_paths.description: Absolute paths the workload may read and write.

AgentGuardrailPolicy.description: Typed guardrail policy attached to a managed agent.

human_inbox.description: When true, fail events route into the unified approval inbox for human review.

name.description:
Unique within the deployment; used in `guardrail_pass`/`guardrail_fail`
events.

tool.description: Optional tool name; when present, the guardrail only fires on that tool.

AgentInstructionFile.description:
One authored instruction file.

Supports inline instruction bodies as well as packaged workload metadata.

allowed_tools.description: Optional tool allowlist carried by skill metadata.

content.description: File body. Packaged workloads should provide the materialized instruction text.

path.description: Original or packaged path, when known.

sha256.description: Optional content digest for packaged workload provenance.

skill_name.description: Skill directory name, when the instruction came from a packaged skill.

AgentInstructionFileSelector.description: Selects instruction files to remove from a managed deployment bundle.

path.description: Original or packaged path. When omitted, every instruction with this kind is selected.

AgentInstructionKind.description: Typed instruction-file kind for managed LLM workloads.

AgentMemoryPolicy.description: Typed memory policy declared by a managed agent.

oneOf.description: Optional context-window compaction policy.
Structured graph memory policy.
Encrypted knowledge retrieval policy for agent-authored instructions.
Semantic / long-term memory policy.

transcript_retention_days.description: Optional retention for raw run transcripts (days).

oneOf.description: Optional convenience preset that expands to a method set when set.

binaries.description: Optional per-binary glob list restricting which processes may use the rule.

enforcement.description: Whether matches enforce or just audit.

methods.description: Optional HTTP methods this rule applies to (rest/sse protocols only).

paths.description: Optional path globs this rule applies to (rest/sse protocols only).

blocked_request_inbox.description:
When true, blocked egress requests are queued for operator approval
(shares an inbox with tool approvals).

blocked_request_max_wait_seconds.description:
Wall-clock cap (in seconds) on how long the runtime will hold a
single blocked-egress request waiting for an operator decision before
surfacing it as `Expired`. When `None`, the runtime applies its
built-in default (30 minutes).

default.description: Default disposition when no rule matches a request.

egress_rules.description: Ordered egress rules. The first matching rule decides the request.

capability_drop.description: Linux capabilities to drop. Defaults to `["ALL"]` at the controller.

run_as_user.description: Optional uid:gid pair (e.g. `1000:1000`) the workload runs under.

seccomp_profile.description: Seccomp profile name; defaults to the runtime default.

AgentRuntimePolicy.description:
Typed runtime policy that a managed agent declares as part of its `AgentSpec`.

The control plane validates this object on create/update; the runtime
controller renders it into Kubernetes objects (security context, resource
limits, NetworkPolicy). Today the controller renders a subset; fields it
does not yet honor remain part of the declared contract so revisions and
rollbacks stay consistent.

oneOf.description:
Optional sandbox runtime class (for example `gvisor` or `kata`).
When omitted, the platform default applies.

version.description: Policy schema version. Must be `1` until a breaking field set is added.

AgentSpec.description: Managed deploy request for the `seren-agent` first-class publisher.

agent_slug.description: Optional stable slug for the agent. If omitted, derived from `name`.

oneOf.description: Optional alert webhook policy.

allowed_remote_agent_origins.description:
Optional allowlist for remote A2A delegation targets.

Entries may be either hostnames (for example `agents.example.com`) or
full origins (for example `https://agents.example.com`).

oneOf.description: Safety policy for publisher operations. Defaults to `read_only`.
Optional runtime capability policy.

credentials.description:
Optional credential references resolved by the control plane at runtime.
Reusable `org-secret://` and `user-secret://` records are managed
through `/organizations/{organization_id}/agent-credential-secrets`.

cron_schedule.description: Cron schedule expression, required when `mode` is `cron`.

cron_timezone.description: Timezone for cron schedules. Defaults to `UTC` when omitted.

oneOf.description: Optional eval gate that must pass before runs proceed.

guardrails.description: Optional guardrail policies applied at input/output/tool boundaries.

oneOf.description: Optional memory policy (semantic memory, transcripts, compaction).
Runtime/model execution profile. Defaults to `balanced`.

name.description: Human-readable agent name. Auto-generated when omitted.

oneOf.description: Output persistence policy for model/tool traces.

Optional typed runtime policy (filesystem, network, process, resources).
Today the controller renders the network section as a NetworkPolicy and
the resources section as Pod resources; other sections are reserved.
Optional external session database configuration for durable agent state.
Opinionated managed template. Defaults to `research_monitor`.

tool_refs.description:
Optional typed tool references. Coexists with `tool_presets` so the
runtime can resolve from typed refs and preset groups together.

workload.description: Runtime workload specification (execution strategy, compute, limits, ...).

AgentSpecUpdate.description:
Partial update request for an existing managed `seren-agent` deployment.

Mirrors the agent-level fields of `AgentSpec` as `Option<...>` for partial
update. The `workload` field replaces the entire `WorkloadSpec` when
present; partial-workload patching is intentionally out of scope.

agent_slug.description: Updated stable slug for the agent.

oneOf.description: Updated alert webhook policy.

allowed_remote_agent_origins.description: Updated allowlist for remote A2A delegation targets.

oneOf.description: Updated approval policy.
Updated capability policy. Replaces the entire previous policy when present.

clear_eval_gate.description: Clear any existing eval gate configuration.

clear_session_database.description: Clear any existing external session database configuration.

clear_tool_refs.description:
Clear any existing typed tool refs and omit `tool_refs` from the
resolved runtime config. Cannot be combined with `tool_refs`.

credentials.description:
Updated credential references. Replaces the entire previous list when
present. Reusable `org-secret://` and `user-secret://` records are
managed through the organization credential-secret API.

cron_schedule.description: Updated cron schedule. Only valid for cron deployments.

cron_timezone.description: Updated cron timezone. Only valid for cron deployments.

oneOf.description: Updated eval gate configuration.

guardrails.description: Updated guardrail list. Replaces the entire previous list when present.

oneOf.description: Updated memory policy. Replaces the entire previous policy when present.
Updated model/runtime profile.
Updated output persistence policy for model/tool traces.
Updated runtime policy. Replaces the entire previous policy when present.
Updated external session database configuration.
Updated managed template.

tool_refs.description:
Updated typed tool refs. Replaces the entire previous list when present.

An empty list is an explicit empty replacement and will serialize as
`tool_refs: []` in runtime config. Use `clear_tool_refs` when the
caller wants to remove the stored typed-tool field entirely.

oneOf.description:
Replaces the entire workload when present. Use the managed files endpoint
for targeted bundle file changes.
Reference to a first-class publisher operation.
Reference to a tool exposed by an MCP server registered with Seren.

Reference to a capability on an organization connector (Gmail, Slack, ...).

`capability == "messaging"` is the generic convention for connectors
that adapt user conversations through a channel such as Telegram, Slack,
SMS, Discord, email, or a publisher-provided messaging surface. Seren
does not route channel traffic from this field. The `connector_ref`
identifies the concrete integration, and `scopes` stays
provider-defined so connector manifests and publisher ecosystems can
evolve without a Seren API enum update.
Reference to a remote agent reachable via A2A.
Reference to a remote HTTP endpoint exposed as a managed tool.

Back-compat shim for the coarse `tool_presets` collections. Presets
are bulk grants and intentionally do not carry action leases.

AgentToolRef.description:
Typed tool reference attached to an agent. Lives alongside the coarser
`tool_presets` field; future managed-agent flows resolve from typed refs and
presets together. Each non-preset variant carries an optional
`permitted_actions` collection of `ActionLease`s — the per-action gate
borrowed from OpenFang/IronClaw that lets the spec say "this Connector may
perform `read` but not `send`" without inventing a separate ACL surface.

CloudDeploymentActionStatusResponse.description: Generic status payload used by cloud deployment lifecycle endpoints.

CloudDeploymentAlertEvent.description: Run-status event that can trigger a deployment alert webhook.

events.description: Run events that should trigger the webhook.

webhook_secret_key.description: Secret key whose value is the destination webhook URL.

CloudDeploymentEvalGateStatus.description: Computed evaluation gate status for a deployment.

CloudDeploymentNetworkPolicy.description: Deployment-level outbound network restrictions for sandboxed execution backends.

allow_list.description: Explicit IPv4 CIDR ranges allowed for outbound access.

block_all.description: When true, block all outbound network access from the deployment runtime.

CloudDeploymentSummary.description: Summary view for listing deployments (excludes large fields like code_bundle).

active_revision_id.description: Current managed-agent revision, when this is a managed seren-agent deployment.

visibility.description: Visibility mode: "open" (default) or "opaque" (redacts selected details from non-owners).

oneOf.description: Optional pagination metadata

data.description: Generic status payload used by cloud deployment lifecycle endpoints.

oneOf.description: Optional pagination metadata

data.description: Summary view for listing deployments (excludes large fields like code_bundle).

active_revision_id.description: Current managed-agent revision, when this is a managed seren-agent deployment.

visibility.description: Visibility mode: "open" (default) or "opaque" (redacts selected details from non-owners).

oneOf.description: Optional pagination metadata

active_revision_id.description: Current managed-agent revision for this deployment.

oneOf.description: Optional runtime capability policy.

conditions.description:
Structured status conditions for the managed deployment. Today this is
synthesized from the flat lifecycle `status` and the last persisted
`error_message`; future work will let the runtime controller report
conditions directly.

credentials.description:
Credential references attached to this deployment, when any. Reusable
`org-secret://` and `user-secret://` records are managed through the
organization credential-secret API.

oneOf.description:
Eval gate that must pass with a fresh verdict before runs proceed.
`set_id` and `max_age_seconds` always travel together; either both are
set or the gate is absent.

guardrails.description: Guardrails attached to this deployment, when any.

oneOf.description: Memory policy declared by the deployment, when set.
Typed runtime policy (filesystem/network/process/resources), when set.

tool_refs.description: Typed tool refs declared on the deployment, when any.

oneOf.description: Optional pagination metadata

Current eval gate status; when `block_on_failure` is set on the
proposed gate and `blocking` is true here, applying this rollback will
be rejected by the control plane.
Optional pagination metadata

Current eval gate status for the proposed gate. When the proposed gate
sets `block_on_failure` and `blocking` is true here, the apply will be
rejected by the control plane.
Optional pagination metadata
Optional pagination metadata
Optional pagination metadata

items.description: Summary view for listing deployments (excludes large fields like code_bundle).

active_revision_id.description: Current managed-agent revision, when this is a managed seren-agent deployment.

visibility.description: Visibility mode: "open" (default) or "opaque" (redacts selected details from non-owners).

oneOf.description: Optional pagination metadata

EvalGate.description:
Eval gate configuration that must pass with a fresh verdict before runs proceed.

`block_on_failure` controls what happens at update or rollback apply time:
when `Some(true)`, the control plane rejects the apply if the latest eval
verdict has failed or has expired beyond `max_age_seconds`. The default
(`None`/`false`) preserves the original freshness-only behavior for
back-compat.

`schedule` is an operator-supplied cron expression that asks the control
plane to run the gated eval set on a fixed cadence so freshness stays
current without manual triggering. The actual scheduling-loop dispatch is
owned by the eval-set scheduler today (see `CloudEvalSetSchedule`); this
field records the operator intent on the deployment side.

`drift_baseline` is captured by the apply path when an update or rollback
commits with a passing gate. Subsequent eval runs compare against the
baseline so operators see regressions explicitly.

oneOf.description:
Eval-set verdict captured at the most recent successful apply. Set by
the control plane; clients may submit this field but the control plane
will overwrite it on every successful apply.
Optional cron-driven cadence for re-running the gated eval set.

EvalGateDriftBaseline.description:
Snapshot of an eval-set verdict captured at the last successful apply.
Subsequent eval runs (manual or scheduled) compare against this snapshot
to surface drift; the apply path overwrites this value on every commit.

EvalGateSchedule.description:
Cron-driven cadence requested by the operator for an eval gate. The
timezone is an IANA name (e.g. `UTC`, `America/New_York`); when omitted the
control plane defaults to UTC.

cron.description:
Standard 5-field cron expression. Validated against the same parser
that backs deployment cron schedules.

timezone.description: Optional IANA timezone. Defaults to `UTC` when absent.

GrantedActions.description:
Capability granted by an `ActionLease`. `All` grants every sub-action of
the named action; `Specific` enumerates a closed set.

active_revision_id.description: Current managed-agent revision for this deployment.

oneOf.description: Optional runtime capability policy.

conditions.description:
Structured status conditions for the managed deployment. Today this is
synthesized from the flat lifecycle `status` and the last persisted
`error_message`; future work will let the runtime controller report
conditions directly.

credentials.description:
Credential references attached to this deployment, when any. Reusable
`org-secret://` and `user-secret://` records are managed through the
organization credential-secret API.

oneOf.description:
Eval gate that must pass with a fresh verdict before runs proceed.
`set_id` and `max_age_seconds` always travel together; either both are
set or the gate is absent.

guardrails.description: Guardrails attached to this deployment, when any.

oneOf.description: Memory policy declared by the deployment, when set.
Typed runtime policy (filesystem/network/process/resources), when set.

tool_refs.description: Typed tool refs declared on the deployment, when any.

oneOf.description:
Current eval gate status; when `block_on_failure` is set on the
proposed gate and `blocking` is true here, applying this rollback will
be rejected by the control plane.

Current eval gate status for the proposed gate. When the proposed gate
sets `block_on_failure` and `blocking` is true here, the apply will be
rejected by the control plane.

database_name.description: Optional Seren organization database name when `provider` is `seren_organization_database`.

engine.description: Session database engine family. The runtime still consumes a standard connection URL.

url_secret_key.description: Secret key whose value is the connection URL for the session database.

ManagedDeploymentCondition.description:
Structured condition reported on a managed deployment's status. Mirrors the
shape of `metav1.Condition` (Kubernetes) in our own DTOs without importing
the Kubernetes types into the public API. Each condition has a typed
`type`, a tri-state `status`, and human-readable `reason`/`message` fields.

last_transition_time.description: Timestamp of the most recent transition into the current `status`.

message.description: Human-readable explanation; safe to surface in operator UI.

reason.description: Short token describing why the condition has its current status.

ManagedDeploymentConditionState.description: Tri-state condition value. Mirrors Kubernetes condition status.

ManagedDeploymentConditionType.description:
Closed set of managed-deployment condition types. New types should be added
here only when there is a clear semantic, not for ad-hoc operator messages.

OrchestrationMode.description:
Cloud deployment orchestration mode.

`Script` (default): container runs user code directly (existing behavior).
`Llm`: container runs the seren-orchestrator LLM tool-calling loop.

count.description: Number of items in this response

has_more.description: Whether there are more items after this page

total.description: Total number of items across all pages

RequirementSource.description: Where a deployment requirement should be satisfied from.

RequirementSpec.description: A declared requirement for a cloud deployment (validated at deploy time).

SideEffectPolicy.description: Deployment-level safety controls for mutating built-in publisher calls.

deployment.description: Draft managed-agent deployment spec to test.

message.description: Optional test message. When omitted, the server uses a generic first-run prompt.

ToolApprovalCondition.description: Condition for a conditional approval rule.

ToolApprovalRule.description: Conditional approval rule for an orchestration tool.

ToolApprovalType.description: Approval policy for an LLM orchestration tool.

ToolDefinition.description: A single tool definition for LLM orchestration.

max_output_bytes.description: Maximum output size in bytes for this tool. Output exceeding this limit is truncated.

timeout_override_seconds.description: Per-tool timeout override in seconds. Overrides the deployment-level tool timeout.

oneOf.description: LLM-orchestrated agent with optional tool definitions and model config.
Runtime adapter used to run the agent loop.

bundle.description: Authored instruction and asset files for the LLM agent.

fallback_models.description: Ordered list of fallback model identifiers tried on primary failure.

oneOf.description: LLM connection override for private-model routing.

model_config.description: Arbitrary model parameters forwarded to the provider.

tool_definitions.description: Tool definitions available to the LLM during execution.

oneOf.description: Code workload deployed from a content-addressed deployment bundle.

deployment_bundle_id.description: Uploaded deployment bundle identifier returned by `seren_cloud_create_deployment_bundle`.

requirements_txt.description: Python requirements.txt contents injected at build time.

oneOf.description: Runtime kind (language/format) for the deployment bundle.

WorkloadExecution.description:
Execution strategy for a workload.

Tagged on the `type` field so the wire shape is `{"type":"llm",...}` or
`{"type":"code",...}`.

WorkloadLimits.description:
Resource and iteration limits applied to a workload run.

All fields default to `None` (no limit).

context_budget_tokens.description: Maximum context window budget in tokens.

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of agent loop iterations before the run is terminated.

max_timeout_seconds.description: Wall-clock timeout in seconds for the entire run.

max_tool_calls_per_run.description: Maximum number of tool calls across the entire run.

max_tool_output_chars.description: Maximum number of characters kept from any single tool output.

WorkloadSpec.description:
Full runtime specification for a cloud workload.

Captures compute backend, network policy, side-effect controls, and the
execution discriminator. The `execution` field is required; all other
fields default to `None` or their zero value.

oneOf.description: Compute backend override. Uses the deployment default when absent.

config.description: Arbitrary operator-supplied configuration passed to the runtime.

execution.description: Execution strategy -- either LLM-orchestrated or artifact-bundle backed.

limits.description: Resource and iteration limits for the workload.

oneOf.description: Outbound network restrictions for sandboxed backends.

publisher_only.description: When true, the workload is exposed only through publisher-managed entry points.

requirements.description: Declared requirements validated at deploy time.

secrets.description: Secret references available to the runtime.

oneOf.description: Controls for mutating built-in publisher calls.
